runs.content:
Chip War: The Fight for the World's Most Critical Technology — Chris Miller (Non-fiction)
Why Fred: FT Business Book of the Year — semiconductors as the geopolitical chokepoint of US-China power; direct match for his TSMC/NVIDIA/HBM supply-chain research in the AI & Tech wiki domain.

Karla's Choice — Nick Harkaway (Spy Thriller)
Why Fred: Le Carré's son writing an authorized new Smiley novel set between The Spy Who Came in from the Cold and Tinker Tailor — reviewers say it reads like "a long-lost le Carré classic"; natural next step after The Constant Gardener.

Number Go Up: Inside Crypto's Wild Rise and Staggering Fall — Zeke Faux (Non-fiction)
Why Fred: Multiple Book of the Year pick (FT, Washington Post); a Bloomberg reporter chases crypto fraud from Sam Bankman-Fried to a Cambodian scam compound — same fraud-detective energy as Red Notice and his short-selling research, aimed at his live crypto portfolio.

Klara and the Sun — Kazuo Ishiguro (Literary Fiction)
Why Fred: Outside territory — Nobel laureate's quietly devastating novel narrated by an AI companion — a literary, character-driven take on the consciousness questions his AI & Tech research keeps circling from the technical side.
